t: Creating a camera experience for the Lock Screen
a: Offer your app’s camera experience on locked devices from Control Center, the Lock Screen, and the Action button.
Capture content with your app’s camera experience when the device is locked.
A protocol that creates a locked camera capture extension.
A protocol that provides the UI for the locked camera capture extension.
An object that provides handling of captured content and transitioning to the extension’s containing app.
URLs provided by the [sessionContentUpdates](/documentation/lockedcameracapture/lockedcameracapturemanager/sessioncontentupdates) `AsyncSequence`.
A URL to a directory of added session content.

URLs to directories of the current session content available when beginning observation of [sessionContentUpdates](/documentation/lockedcameracapture/lockedcameracapturemanager/sessioncontentupdates).
A URL to a directory of removed session content.
An object that can request to open the extension’s containing app and receives session configuration updates.
Indicates why launching the extension’s containing app failed.
An error that the launch failed because the system didn’t find the application.
An error that the launch failed because authentication failed and the device is locked.
An error that the launch failed with an unknown error.
A structure that contains the session object and UI to display for the locked camera capture extension.
Project version number for LockedCameraCapture.
Project version string for LockedCameraCapture.
A type to use when opening your app from the capture extension.
Tells the system that the application wants to delay the application launch during a transition between the extension and the application
The content for the locked camera capture extension.
The content and behavior of the locked camera capture extension’s UI.
Tells the system that the application is ready to appear after delaying the appearance during a transition between the extension and the application
An integer value that represents the error code.
The domain for errors that can occur when launching the extension’s containing app.
A string that describes the error that occurred.
Creates a locked camera capture extension scene.

Invalidates the contents of the session contents URL, deleting them. Note this does not remove the contents directory itself. This is useful in case the extension has already ingested its contents via PhotoKit and wishes to not persist any data (but can still use this directory as a working directory to recover from an unexpected termination).
Tells the system that the app no longer needs the directory at the URL and it can be deleted.
Initiates a request to open the extension’s containing app.
An object that can request to open the extension’s containing app and receives session configuration updates.
A temporary directory URL inside the extension’s data container.
An array of URLs that each point to a directory containing captured content.
An `AsyncSequence` to process captured content from your capture extension.
The shared instance of the manager.
